title: Seer Evidence Summary (Lock B)
description: The evidence basis for diagnosis and prediction. Captures key findings, sources, coverage assessment, critical gaps, and proxy evidence. This is Lock B — the diagnostic cannot introduce claims beyond what is locked here.

key_findings.description: The most important findings from the research phase. Each finding should be a specific, sourced claim — not a summary or impression.

source.description: Source identifier. E.g., 'Tesla 10-K FY2025', 'Bloomberg consensus March 2026'.

confidence.description: Confidence in the source's reliability and relevance.

signal_type.description: Primary: direct observation or official data. Secondary: analysis or reporting on primary data. Proxy: indirect evidence requiring transfer assumptions.

coverage_band.description: How much of the system's SIRF channels are covered by available evidence.

critical_gaps.description: What the evidence doesn't cover. Honest gap inventory. Each gap should specify which channel or dynamic is unobserved.

type.description: Category of proxy. E.g., 'analogous system', 'adjacent metric', 'lagged indicator'.

description.description: What the proxy evidence is and where it comes from.

transfer_conditions.description: Under what conditions this proxy transfers to the target system. What would break the transfer.

proxy_evidence.description: Indirect evidence used when direct observation is unavailable. Must declare transfer conditions.

contradictions.description: Evidence items that contradict each other or the emerging diagnosis. Surfacing these is a feature, not a bug.

decision.description: Whether the evidence is sufficient to proceed to diagnosis.

rationale.description: Why the decision was made. If go_with_caveats, what the caveats are.
